transcript.clean_text: the wide open Plains and Olive Groves of Spain beckoning us for stage 13 of the Welter Richard karapas having drizzled some love over Indian grenadiers With Victory the day previously to penius Blancas where we'd witness evident Paul coming home ahead of Mass androglich to maintain his dominance of this race his leading margin at the moment in the red jersey competition two minutes and 41. and so what lay ahead today the possibility of some wind but that would be a Tailwind plenty of entertainment for everyone who comes along to the start House of any stage and a man who looked like he was ready for action John Degan called who'd won on the very ramps that finish this day back in 2014. well would it be the pure fast men or a strong finisher the rolling terrain suggested the latter opting to wear some gloves on this day after taking a tumble he with a rough night but not too many bumps and bruises said he would be unaffected for this run to montia 168.4 kilometers by no means a short day and with over 1700 meters of climbing along the way you could tell that much of the terrain would roll and at the end some ramps to contend with sections of which seven percent so hardly one for Pure sprinters would it tempt an enormous Breakaway that was the big question we'd have 32 Riders up the road on stage 12. for stage 13 just three well they've been in the break before Vandenberg okamika and Jan Buu today it felt like the Short Straw never allowed more than three and a half minutes of an advantage the chase was maintained by of course the sprinters teams and as we got closer to our finale the pace picked up Trek segafredo rarely kicked over The Hornet's Nest for mads Pederson wanted to make it difficult as if to prove they were equal to the task borough hansgrove of course working for Danny band Popple and helps into kernick for timberlia joined the fun the intermediate Sprint came very close to the Finish 14 kilometers from home by the time we reached underneath the 10K to go marker the pace was hardly the highest that was due to a headwind that had picked up so it was very cagey with an extremely compact Peloton as we got closer and closer to the line the Breakaway long absorbed and now well it was going to be a war particularly to reach the final corner and then head up the hill Primus ruglich was hunting for some time gaps out there every second counts after all Timberland just boxed and bruised away from his lead out man vermeesh and Rob Stannard and then we took the turn and started our climb who would be first to bite that was the big question there was a lot moving into position again to Bashar for grupama Francis de Gea the green jersey being guided up securely by Alex Kirsch Ryan Mullen also doing fantastic work for Danny Van Poppel for Borah hansgroer but the crucial Corner was beckoning and as you can see the green jersey of mass medicine was going to do this now on his own first a break though was Pascal ackermann from UA team Emirates went very very long hit the climb first and only Matt Peterson could stay with him rushing into the Aero pocket provided by Ackerman Mass Peterson came around his man rock and roll to the line and indeed got the stage win that had been so elusive to him throughout this race Brian Coker fast finishing just coming by Ackerman who was showing his disappointment Fred Wright from Bahrain Victorious finishing in fourth place a dramatic finish and a worthy winner paying his team back for all the hard work that they have produced throughout this race mattersen absolutely in charge of this green jersey the winning margin huge enough to think about celebrations and how he would deliver them and he did it beautifully Patterson taking ahead of ackermann Fred Wright Danny van Popple kenty Bashir eskara van gels Primos roglich they were all there still in control overall Remco Eva Nepal we head for The High Ground this weekend and surely he will face a lot of pressure at the moment the margin is a handsome one will that diminish this weekend well first of two mountains days is stage 14 to La Pandera the climbs back stacked at the end of the day another uphill finish which will provide plenty of drama it's always the case I love Welter join us to see how it pans out
